author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XIV. 7–10.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words of the holy Gospel, brethren, are rightly understood only if they are found to be in harmony with those that precede; for the premises ought to agree with the conclusion, when it is the Truth that speaks.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord had said before, “And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also:” and then had added, “And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know;” and showed that all He said was that they knew Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, therefore, the meaning was of His going to Himself by Himself,—for He also lets the disciples see that it is by Him that they are to come to Him,—we have already told you, as we could, in our last discourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He says, therefore, “That where I am, there ye may be also,” where else were they to be but in Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this way is He also in Himself, and they, therefore, are just where He is, that is, in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, He Himself is that eternal life which is yet to be ours, when He has received us unto Himself; and as He is that life eternal, so is it in Him, that where He is there shall we be also, that is to say, in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For as the Father hath life in Himself,” and certainly that life which He has is in no wise different from what He is Himself as its possessor, “so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself,” inasmuch as He is the very life which He hath in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But shall we then actually be what He is, (namely), the life, when we shall have begun our existence in that life, that is, in Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly not, for He, by His very existence as the life, hath life, and is Himself what He hath; and as the life, is in Him, so is He in Himself: but we are not that life, but partakers of His life, and shall be there in such wise as to be wholly incapable of being in ourselves what He is, but so as, while ourselves not the life, to have Him as our life, who has Himself the life on this very account that He Himself is the life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, He both exists unchangeably in Himself and inseparably in the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we, when wishing to exist in ourselves, were thrown into inward trouble regarding ourselves, as is expressed in the words, “My soul is cast down within me:” and changing from bad to worse, cannot even remain as we were.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when by Him we come unto the Father, according to His own words, “No man cometh unto the Father but by me,” and abide in Him, no one shall be able to separate us either from the Father or from Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Connecting, therefore, His previous words with those that follow, He proceeded to say, “If ye had known me, ye should certainly have known my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This conforms to His previous words, “No man cometh unto the Father but by me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He adds: “And from henceforth ye know Him, and have seen Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Philip, one of the apostles, not understanding what he had just heard, said, “Lord, show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord replied to him, “Have I been so long time with you, and yet have ye not known me, Philip? he that seeth me, seeth also the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you see He complains that He had been so long time with them, and yet He was not known.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But had He not Himself said, “And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know;” and on their saying that they knew it not, had convinced them that they did know, by adding the words: “I am the way, and the truth, and the life”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, says He now, “Have I been so long time with you, and have ye not known me?” when, in fact, they knew both whither He went and the way, on no other grounds save that they really knew Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this difficulty is easily solved by saying that some of them knew Him, and others did not, and that Philip was one of those who did not know Him; so that, when He said, “And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know,” He is understood as having spoken to those that knew, and not to Philip, who has it said to him, “Have I been so long time with you, and have ye not known me, Philip?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To such, then, as already knew the Son, was it now also said of the Father, “And from henceforth ye know Him, and have seen Him:” for such words were used because of the all-sided likeness subsisting between the Father and the Son; so that, because they knew the Son, they might henceforth be said to know the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already, therefore, they knew the Son, if not all of them, those at least to whom it is said, “And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know;” for He is Himself the way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they knew not the Father, and so have also to hear, “If ye have known me, ye have known my Father also;” that is, through me ye have known Him also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I am one, and He another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that they might not think Him unlike, He adds, “And from henceforth ye know Him, and have seen Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they saw His perfectly resembling Son, but needed to have the truth impressed on them, that exactly such as was the Son whom they saw, was the Father also whom they did not see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to this points what is afterwards said to Philip, “He that seeth me, seeth also the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that He Himself was Father and Son, which is a notion of the Sabellians, who are also called Patripassians, condemned by the Catholic faith; but that Father and Son are so alike, that he who knoweth one knoweth both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are accustomed to speak in this way of two who closely resemble each other, to those who are in the habit of seeing one of them, and wish to know what like the other is, so that we say, In seeing the one, you have seen the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this way, then, is it said “He that seeth me, seeth also the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, certainly, that He who is the Son is also the Father, but that the Son in no respect disagrees with the likeness of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had not the Father and Son been two persons, it would not have been said, “If ye have known me, ye have known my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such is certainly the case for “no one,” He says, “cometh unto the Father but by me: if ye have known me, ye have known my Father also;” because it is I, who am the only way to the Father, that will lead you to Him, that He also may Himself become known to you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as I am in all respects His perfect image, “from henceforth ye know Him” in knowing me; “and have seen Him,” if you have seen me with the spiritual eyesight of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, Philip, dost thou say, “Show us the Father, and it sufficeth us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have I been so long time with you, and yet have ye not known me, Philip?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that seeth me, seeth the Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it interests thee much to see this, believe at least what thou seest not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “how,” He says, “sayest thou, Show us the Father?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast seen me, who am His perfect likeness, thou hast seen Him to whom I am like.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if thou canst not directly see this, “believest thou not,” at least, “that I am in the Father, and the Father in me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Philip might say here, “I see Thee indeed, and believe Thy full likeness to the Father; but is one to be reproved and rebuked because, when he sees one who bears a likeness to another, he wishes to see that other to whom he is like?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I know, indeed, the image, but as yet I know only the one without the other; it is not enough for me, unless I know that other whose likeness he bears.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show us, therefore, the Father, and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Master really reproved the disciple because He saw into the heart of his questioner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was with the idea, as if the Father were somehow better than the Son, that Philip had the desire to know the Father: and so he did not even know the Son, because believing that He was inferior to another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was to correct such a notion that it was said, “He that seeth me, seeth the Father also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How sayest thou, Show us the Father?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see the meaning of thy words: it is not the original likeness thou seekest to see, but it is that other thou thinkest the superior.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why desirest thou to dis cover some distance between those who are thus alike? why cravest thou the separate knowledge of those who cannot be separated?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, after this, He says not only to Philip, but to all of them together, must not now be thrust into a corner, in order that, by His help, it may be the more carefully expounded.
